_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-13 Pain Management (Revised 11/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: Procedure (All EMS Levels — scope varies by medication)
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-13: Pain Management — MDHHS Approved 11/17/2025
purpose: Provide standardized prehospital pain assessment and pharmacological management across provider levels. Basic non-pharmacological interventions are always first. Opioids require SpO2 and capnography monitoring. MCAs must select at least one non-opioid and at least one opioid medication.

sections.title: Pain Assessment
points: Use the Wong Pain Scale (0–10):
— 0: No pain
— 1–3: Minor pain
— 4–6: Moderate pain
— 7–10: Severe pain
Reassess pain score after each intervention
Contact Medical Control for patients with established pain management care plans

sections.title: Basic (Non-Pharmacological) Interventions
points: Positioning of comfort
Splinting of injured extremity
Application of ice or cold pack
Emotional support and reassurance
These interventions should be initiated before or concurrent with pharmacological treatment

sections.title: Non-Opioid Analgesics — Adult (MCA Must Select ≥ 1)
points: ACETAMINOPHEN 650 mg PO — adults; EMT and above
IBUPROFEN 400 mg PO — adults; NOT for pregnant patients; EMT and above
KETOROLAC 15 mg IM/IV — adults; NOT for pregnant patients; Specialist and above
KIDNEY STONE PAIN: ketorolac is first-line analgesic if available

sections.title: Non-Opioid Analgesics — Pediatric
points: ACETAMINOPHEN (Children's Elixir 160 mg/5 mL): 15 mg/kg PO — per Children's Elixir Dosing Table
IBUPROFEN (Children's Elixir 100 mg/5 mL): 10 mg/kg PO — per Children's Elixir Dosing Table; NOT for infants ≤ 6 months
KETOROLAC for children > 5 years: 0.5 mg/kg IM/IV; maximum 15 mg

sections.title: Ketamine — Dissociative Analgesic (MCA Selection Required)
points: MCA must select one of three authorization options: Not permitted / Medical Control required prior to administration / Administer per protocol
ADULT IV/IO: 0.2 mg/kg diluted in 100 mL NS; infuse over 5–10 minutes; maximum 25 mg; repeat ONCE ≥ 10 minutes after initial dose
ADULT IN: 0.5 mg/kg; maximum 50 mg; repeat ONCE ≥ 10 minutes after initial dose
PEDIATRIC < 6 years: IN route ONLY; 0.5 mg/kg; per ketamine pediatric dosing table
PEDIATRIC dosing per MI-MEDIC or ketamine pediatric dosing table (Purple through Green zones; > 36 kg = Black/adult)
Monitoring: if severe pain AND ketamine OR opioid administered — continuous SpO2 AND capnography REQUIRED throughout
IV/IO administration must be slow (over 5–10 minutes) — rapid IV administration causes respiratory depression
IM administration: single dose only; no repeat
Contact Medical Control BEFORE administering opioids if ketamine has already been given

sections.title: Opioid Analgesics (MCA Must Select ≥ 1)
points: Adult SBP must be > 100 mmHg before opioid administration
Pediatric SBP must be > 80 + (2 × age) mmHg before opioid administration
Continuous SpO2 AND capnography monitoring REQUIRED with any opioid administration
MORPHINE 0.1 mg/kg IV/IO/IM (maximum 5 mg per dose):
— IV/IO: may repeat up to 3 times; maximum cumulative dose 20 mg; minimum 10 minutes between doses
— IM: single dose only; no repeat
— NOT for patients < 18 months of age
FENTANYL 1 mcg/kg weight-rounded per adult weight table (adult ≤ 25 kg = 25 mcg; 26–50 kg = 50 mcg; 51–75 kg = 75 mcg; ≥ 76 kg = 100 mcg):
— IV/IO/IN: may repeat ONCE; maximum cumulative dose 200 mcg
— IM: single dose only; no repeat
— Pediatric: per MI-MEDIC or 1 mcg/kg

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize at least one non-opioid analgesic for prehospital pain management
options: Ketorolac 15 mg IM/IV (adult, NOT pregnant — Specialist+)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select the level of authorization for sub-dissociative ketamine in pain management
options: Medical Control required prior to administration

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize at least one opioid for prehospital pain management
options: Morphine 0.1 mg/kg IV/IO/IM (max 5 mg; IV/IO repeat x3 max 20 mg; NOT < 18 months)
Fentanyl weight-rounded per table (IV/IO/IN repeat once max 200 mcg; IM no repeat)

key_drugs.name: Ketorolac (Adult Pain)
key_drugs.dose: 15 mg IM or IV; pediatric > 5 years: 0.5 mg/kg IM/IV max 15 mg
key_drugs.indication: Moderate to severe pain; first-line for kidney stone pain
key_drugs.contraindications: Pregnant patients; active GI bleed; renal insufficiency (caution)
key_drugs.side_effects: GI irritation, renal effects with long-term use
key_drugs.notes: NSAIDs: ketorolac is first-line for kidney stone pain if available. Not for pregnant patients.

key_drugs.name: Ketamine IV/IO (Sub-dissociative Analgesia)
key_drugs.dose: 0.2 mg/kg IV/IO diluted in 100 mL NS over 5–10 min; max 25 mg; repeat once ≥ 10 min; IN: 0.5 mg/kg max 50 mg
key_drugs.indication: Moderate to severe pain per MCA selection
key_drugs.contraindications: Per MCA selection; IM = single dose no repeat; peds < 6 yr = IN route only; contact MC before opioids after ketamine
key_drugs.side_effects: Emergence phenomena, nausea, laryngospasm (with rapid IV)
key_drugs.notes: Slow IV push mandatory to avoid respiratory depression. Continuous SpO2 and capnography required. Contact MC before giving opioids after ketamine.

key_drugs.name: Morphine (Pain)
key_drugs.dose: 0.1 mg/kg IV/IO/IM; max 5 mg per dose; IV/IO repeat x3 max 20 mg cumulative; IM no repeat
key_drugs.indication: Moderate to severe pain — adult SBP > 100; peds SBP > 80+(2×age)
key_drugs.contraindications: SBP < 100 (adult); not for patients < 18 months; IM = no repeat
key_drugs.notes: NOT for patients < 18 months. Continuous SpO2 and capnography required. IV/IO may repeat up to 3 times minimum 10 min apart, max 20 mg.

key_drugs.name: Fentanyl (Pain)
key_drugs.dose: 1 mcg/kg weight-rounded: ≤ 25 kg = 25 mcg; 26–50 kg = 50 mcg; 51–75 kg = 75 mcg; ≥ 76 kg = 100 mcg; IV/IO/IN repeat once max 200 mcg; IM no repeat
key_drugs.indication: Moderate to severe pain — adult SBP > 100; peds SBP > 80+(2×age)
key_drugs.contraindications: SBP below threshold; IM = single dose no repeat
key_drugs.side_effects: Respiratory depression, chest wall rigidity (rapid IV), hypotension
key_drugs.notes: Pediatric: per MI-MEDIC or 1 mcg/kg. Continuous SpO2 and capnography required. IV/IO/IN may repeat once max 200 mcg.

deterioration_flags: SBP < 100 (adult) — do not administer opioids; treat hypotension per appropriate protocol
SpO2 decreasing after opioid — assist ventilation; consider naloxone per Opioid Overdose Protocol
Respiratory depression after ketamine rapid IV push — slow infusion is required; assist ventilation as needed
Ketamine given — contact Medical Control BEFORE giving opioids
scope_notes: Acetaminophen PO, Ibuprofen PO: EMT and above (per MCA selection)
Ketorolac IM/IV: Specialist and above (per MCA selection)
Morphine IV/IO/IM: per MCA selection; not for < 18 months
Opioids: continuous SpO2 and capnography required; SBP thresholds must be met
Ketamine + opioid: contact MC before giving opioid; continuous SpO2 and capnography required
